header.headline: Q4 K Superblock
header.subtitle: Superblock diagrams need to expose how grouped blocks, scales, and metadata travel together.

stage_1.title: Source Values
stage_1.body: Q4 K Superblock starts from packed or full-precision source values that define the numeric contract.

stage_2.title: Packing Rule
stage_2.body: The central rule decides which bits survive and how dropped detail is accounted for in q4 k superblock.

stage_3.title: Block Layout
stage_3.body: Storage layout turns that rule into a concrete block or lane shape the runtime can consume.

stage_4.title: Runtime Decode
stage_4.body: The final behavior depends on decode order, scale recovery, and the remaining error envelope.

left.title: Structure View
left.body: Show the concrete packed structure the runtime reads for q4 k superblock.

right.title: Runtime Effect
right.body: Explain the throughput, precision, or memory consequence of the chosen layout.

footer.note: Q4 K Superblock belongs in the broader contract because the model needs dense technical layouts, not only charts and posters.
